| the mystery of faith is great
The Church is without beginning, without end and eternal, just as the
Triune God, her founder, is without beginning, without end and eternal.

E She is uncreated just as God is uncreated. She existed before the ages, : before the angels, before the creation of the world — before the founda: tion of the world as the Apostle Paul says. She is a divine institution and Eph. 1:4 E in her dwells the whole fullness of divinity. She is an expression of the Col. 2:9 - richly varied wisdom of God. She is the mystery of mysteries. She was 7 concealed and was revealed in the last of times. The Church remains un- 1 Pet. 1:20 E shaken because she is rooted in the love and wise providence of God. | ‘The three persons of the Holy Trinity constitute the eternal Church. i The angels and human beings existed in the thought and love of the TriF une God from the beginning. We human beings were not born now, we E existed before the ages in God's omniscience. | The love of God created us in His image and likeness. He embraced © us within the Church in spite of the fact that He knew of our apostasy. | He gave us everything to make us gods too through the free gift of grace.

E For all that, we made poor use of our freedom and lost our original . beauty, our original righteousness and cut ourselves off from the Church. & Qutside the Church, far from the Holy Trinity, we lost Paradise, every| thing. But outside the Church there is no salvation, there is no life. And i s0 the compassionate heart of God the Father did not leave us exiled | from His love. He opened again for us the gates of Paradise in the last iv of times and appeared in flesh. ¥ With the divine incarnation of the only-begotten Son of God, Gods f pre-eternal plan for the salvation of mankind was revealed again to | men. In his epistle to Timothy the Apostle Paul says: Incontrovertably, - the mystery of faith is great. God was revealed in flesh, justified in Spirit, . seen by angels, preached among the gentiles, believed in throughout the | world, taken up in glory. The words of the Apostle Paul are dense in 1 Tim. 3:16 2 meaning: divine, heavenly words! ge God in His infinite love united us again with His Church in the person of Christ.
